title: WIP: rough style modulation idea with smoke test
author: jxning0422-dev
body:
## Summary

This PR explores a set of inductive biases aimed at improving compression-oriented language modeling beyond standard next-token prediction.

The core idea is to help the model better adapt to local structure, reuse nearby context efficiently, and capture medium-scale sequence patterns that may be underemphasized by token-level loss alone.

## Strategy

The work focuses on three complementary directions:

- **Style modulation**  
  Introduces a lightweight pathway that summarizes recent context, allowing the model to adapt to shifting local statistics (e.g., formatting, punctuation, lexical patterns). This helps the model operate in different “modes” of text more effectively.

- **Frequency-domain loss**  
  Adds an auxiliary loss to encourage better modeling of medium-scale structure in sequences. This is intended to complement token-level loss by improving how the model captures variation and patterns over longer spans.

- **Local memory**  
  Adds mechanisms for emphasizing nearby dependencies and carrying forward compact summaries across layers. The goal is to reuse local context more efficiently rather than recomputing it via full attention at every layer.

## Current picture

This is still a work in progress, but a few patterns are emerging:

- The strongest results so far come from:
  - **frequency loss (mid-range band)**  
  - **dual-style summary with gating**

- **Gating appears consistently beneficial**, especially in the newer style variants.

- **Basic style modulation provides a small but consistent improvement** over a no-style baseline, with simple FiLM-style modulation performing best among earlier variants.

- **Local memory is still inconclusive** and requires more complete runs.

## Status

⚠️ This is still preliminary:

- Results are based on partial sweeps and limited seeds
- No final conclusions yet
- Some directions (especially local memory) are still running

## Next steps

- Complete full runs for local memory variants
- Validate top configurations across seeds
- Quantify gains vs. added complexity
- Identify the simplest variant that delivers consistent improvements

## Notes

This PR is intended to share the direction early and get feedback before committing to a more finalized approach.
